title: Tailwind CSS Mastery
description: Master Tailwind CSS — utility-first concepts, responsive design, flex/grid, theming, plugins, and dark mode.

modules.title: Utility-First Fundamentals
modules.description: Core utility-first philosophy, spacing, colors, typography.

sections.title: Utility-First Concept
sections.body_markdown: What is the 'utility-first' approach in Tailwind CSS?

choices.A: Writing CSS in a separate utility file
choices.B: Using small, single-purpose CSS classes directly in HTML

sections.explanation: Utility-first means composing designs with small, focused classes like `text-center`, `p-4`, `bg-blue-500` directly in markup.

sections.title: Spacing Scale
sections.explanation: Tailwind's spacing scale: each unit = 0.25rem. So `p-4` = 1rem (16px) padding, `m-2` = 0.5rem (8px) margin.

sections.title: Color System

choices.B: The shade/intensity on a 50–950 scale (500 = medium)

sections.explanation: Tailwind uses a numeric shade scale (50=lightest, 950=darkest). 500 is the medium/default shade.

sections.title: Typography
sections.explanation: `leading-tight` sets `line-height: 1.25`, creating a compact vertical rhythm. In Tailwind, 'leading' = line-height.

modules.title: Responsive & Layout
modules.description: Responsive prefixes, flexbox, grid, and layout patterns.

sections.title: Responsive Prefixes
sections.body_markdown: How do Tailwind's responsive prefixes work?

choices.A: They apply styles below the breakpoint
choices.B: They apply styles at and above the breakpoint (mobile-first)
choices.C: They only apply at exactly that breakpoint

sections.explanation: Tailwind is mobile-first: `md:` applies at ≥768px and up. Unprefixed classes are the mobile default.

sections.title: Flexbox

choices.B: Horizontal row, vertically centered, space between items, 1rem gap

sections.explanation: `flex` = horizontal row, `items-center` = vertical centering, `justify-between` = space between, `gap-4` = 1rem gap.

sections.title: Grid
sections.explanation: `grid-cols-3` creates 3 equal columns using `grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr))`.

sections.title: Container & Max-Width

choices.B: Sets max-width to the current breakpoint's width and centers with mx-auto
choices.C: Adds a border around the content

sections.explanation: `container` sets `max-width` matching the current responsive breakpoint. `mx-auto` centers it horizontally.

modules.title: Customization & Dark Mode
modules.description: Custom themes, @apply, plugins, and dark mode.

sections.title: @apply Directive

choices.B: Extracts Tailwind utility classes into a custom CSS class

sections.explanation: `@apply` lets you compose Tailwind utilities into a single reusable class in your CSS file.

sections.title: Extending the Theme
sections.body_markdown: After this config, which class would you use?
sections.explanation: Custom colors in `extend.colors` generate utility classes: `text-brand`, `bg-brand`, `border-brand`, etc.

sections.title: Dark Mode
sections.body_markdown: How does Tailwind's `dark:` prefix work by default?

choices.A: It checks the user's system preference via prefers-color-scheme
choices.C: It requires a dark class on the html element (class strategy)
choices.D: Both A and C are valid strategies depending on configuration

sections.explanation: Tailwind supports both `media` (system preference) and `class` (manual toggle) dark mode strategies, configurable in tailwind.config.js.

sections.title: Plugins
sections.body_markdown: How do you add custom utilities or components to Tailwind?

choices.A: Edit the source code of Tailwind
choices.B: Write a plugin function in tailwind.config.js using the plugin API

sections.explanation: Tailwind's plugin API lets you register custom utilities, components, and base styles via `addUtilities`, `addComponents`, etc.
